behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between formula parsing, range resolution, and built-in function execution over workbook cell state. A pure unit test of `SUM` alone can miss range-binding bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 to known numeric values, set a target cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run the normal evaluation pipeline, and assert the computed value equals the arithmetic sum.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This bug appears at module boundaries: dependency resolution plus multi-cell evaluation order. A unit test of arithmetic or single-formula eval is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: In an in-memory sheet, set `A1=3`, `B1==A1*2`, and `C1==B1+1`; evaluate workbook and assert `B1=6` and `C1=7` through the normal dependency engine.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection must be exercised through real reference graph construction and evaluator orchestration. Unit-testing only the graph algorithm can miss integration defects in how edges are built.
behaviors.test_description: Set `A1==B1+1` and `B1==A1+1` in a workbook, invoke standard recomputation, assert a circular-reference error object/code is returned for affected cells, and assert evaluation terminates promptly (no hang).

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often occur in table-range extraction plus function semantics (exact-match mode, column indexing, not-found propagation), which cross module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table range in cells, evaluate a `VLOOKUP(..., FALSE)` formula for an existing key and a missing key via the full evaluator, and assert correct return value and correct not-found error token/code.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: The core failure mode is expression/function semantics for nested conditionals; no external I/O or mandatory multi-module boundary is required.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the nested IF expression against a small evaluation context with `A1` values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high` respectively.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily function-level string handling logic (argument normalization and empty-value policy). Integration wiring is not the minimum needed to expose the defect.
behaviors.test_description: Call CONCATENATE evaluation with representative argument lists including empty-cell values and separators, and assert exact output strings without extra separators/blanks.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Cross-sheet resolution depends on workbook/sheet namespace wiring plus evaluator lookup. A single-function unit test can miss sheet-binding bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with `Sheet1` and `Sheet2`, set `Sheet2!A1`, place `=Sheet2!A1+1` in `Sheet1`, run normal evaluation, and assert the computed value matches expectation.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is specifically about dependency graph propagation and scheduler ordering across cells, which is an inter-module coordination failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/diamond (e.g., A1 -> B1,C1 -> D1), evaluate once, mutate A1, trigger incremental recalculation, and assert all downstream cells reflect new values with no stale/intermediate artifacts.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness spans edit history, formula storage, dependency invalidation, and recomputation. The failure appears when these modules interact, not in isolated logic.
behaviors.test_description: Start with dependent formulas evaluated, edit an upstream formula, confirm changed downstream values, execute undo through the public undo stack, and assert original formula text and dependent computed values are fully restored.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real file I/O and binary serialization/deserialization against OS filesystem behavior; mocking persistence can hide real corruption/encoding issues.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with formulas and edge-case floating-point values, save to an actual temporary file, reload through production load path, and assert exact formula text equality plus numeric equality within strict precision policy (and bit-exact where required).
